title: Setup Guide
intro: Wire the running mimo2codex into Codex CLI / desktop. The snippets below are generated live from the current host:port — copy and paste as-is.

auth.warn: This overwrites your existing <1>{{path}}</1>. If you also sign into a real OpenAI account in Codex, use option 2 (env-key) or option 3 (cc-switch) instead.
auth.step3Title: Step 3 · Fully quit and restart Codex
auth.step3Body: Codex desktop must fully quit (click Quit in the tray/menubar icon) so it re-reads auth.json on next launch. Then choose <1>{{label}}</1> as the provider inside Codex.

preserve.info: Keeps your existing <1>{{path}}</1> (your Sign in with ChatGPT login) completely untouched — only config.toml is changed to route the model through mimo2codex. Use this so OpenAI's official Codex mobile/remote keeps working.
preserve.note: Your ChatGPT OAuth token stays in auth.json and is attached to proxy requests via requires_openai_auth = true; mimo2codex ignores it and forwards to your real upstream. To drive this Codex from your phone, set up OpenAI's official 'Codex mobile' in the desktop app — it needs the very login this option preserves.

envkey.info: This option leaves your existing <1>{{path}}</1> untouched and authenticates via the <3>MIMO2CODEX_KEY</3> env var. <5>CLI only</5> — Codex desktop launched from Finder/Start Menu doesn't inherit shell env.
envkey.note: Any non-empty string works — mimo2codex doesn't validate inbound credentials. Your real upstream keys (MIMO_API_KEY, DS_API_KEY, ...) stay in the environment of the machine running mimo2codex.

ccswitch.info: <1>cc-switch</1> is a Codex provider switcher desktop app. Open it, <3>Add Provider → Codex tab → Custom</3>, paste each snippet below into the matching input.
ccswitch.note: OPENAI_API_KEY can be any non-empty string — mimo2codex doesn't validate inbound credentials. Real upstream keys stay on the mimo2codex host.
